danger_signals.visual_form: Animated rising/falling magma in Cave of Wonders stage
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate environmental hazard requiring upward/downward traversal to avoid

danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile creatures positioned throughout platforming sections
danger_signals.meaning: Combat encounters requiring jump-on or apple-throw mechanics to neutralize

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen bottom boundary in all stages
danger_signals.meaning: Bottomless pit death state—loss of life if player falls below screen

danger_signals.visual_form: Heart meter UI element decreasing on hit
danger_signals.meaning: Cumulative damage from enemy contact or obstacle collision

danger_signals.visual_form: Large hand made of fire (unused sprite 0x71)
danger_signals.meaning: Planned Stage 6 hazard—fire-based threat with directional facing and patrol behavior

safety_signals.visual_form: Sparkle/gleam of collectible gems scattered in stages
safety_signals.meaning: Resource pickup granting extra lives and score; red gems provide substantial score boost
Bonus stage gateway—collect scarab within time window to access Genie wheel for extra lives and power-ups

safety_signals.visual_form: Heart items appearing after defeating enemies or in bonus stages
Self-scrolling flying stage with open sky environment
safety_signals.meaning: Safe traversal zone with music-driven timing (Jasmine bonus stage ends with 'A Whole New World' melody)
Player can resume from current level if continues remain—failure is recoverable

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Street urchin protagonist with monkey companion Abu
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stages progress through Agrabah city locations; narrative beats tied to Disney film sequence

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Non-specific platformer enemies defeated via jump-on or apple-throw
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy placement varies by stage; Cave of Wonders has lava-themed hazards; later stages feature bosses

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Wheel-spinning mechanic granting rewards (extra lives, power-ups)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bonus stages accessible only through scarab collection in treasure chests

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct stage environments reflecting film locations: street scenes, Cave of Wonders, palace areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy types and hazard configurations change per stage; bosses appear at stages 1, 6, 7; stage 7 is final confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Animated lava waves rising/falling as visual threat indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Self-scrolling forced navigation up and down to outrun advancing lava—time pressure mechanic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Free-flying environment with open sky, collectible gems scattered
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Music-driven duration—stage ends when 'A Whole New World' melody concludes, not on player failure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Heart meter depletes visually with each hit; game over when all lives consumed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced survivability increases tension; continue system allows retry from current stage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dedicated boss sprite and arena design (stages 1, 6, 7)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy does not follow generic patrol—executes scripted attack patterns requiring player dodge/counter

environmental_storytelling.example: Stages unfold through Agrabah locations (city streets → Cave of Wonders → palace), mirroring Disney film plot sequence without explicit exposition
Lava in Cave of Wonders stage visually communicates magical danger; Magic Carpet stage uses open sky to signal escape/freedom
Scarab scarab spawning from chests establishes collectible reward system tied to bonus access—teaches player world mechanics through interactive discovery

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy visual variety implying setting specificity
environmental_storytelling.example: Planned unused Fire Hand enemy (Stage 6) would have communicated magical/supernatural threat type without dialogue

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Jumps on enemies or throws apples to disorient; apple throw indicates ranged option vs. melee jump
Follows Aladdin; mechanically integrated into player control (not specified in sources but present in gameplay)
Defeat via jump-on or apple disorientation; implies simple AI following patrol routes until player interaction
Scripted attack patterns (not random patrol); stages 1, 6, 7 feature boss-specific arenas implying designed encounters
Three behaviors: facing right, facing left, moving back-and-forth in small area; suggests melee threat with telegraphed direction
Blue platform travels 8 screens at constant pace; red platform travels twice as long with increasing speed acceleration—implies dynamic difficulty
Waves advance and recede; forces player upward/downward navigation; implies mechanical pressure system vs. static hazard

notes: This game contains a strong Disney film adaptation narrative structure with visual world-state tied to stage progression rather than dynamic faction conflict. Danger signals are primarily environmental (lava, bottomless pits, enemies) rather than political or faction-based. Safety is communicated through collectible rewards and bonus access mechanics. The unused enemies (Fire Hand, Moving Platforms) suggest originally planned variety. No explicit faction conflict or political messaging detected in available sources—world-state is primarily adventure/exploration with escalating difficulty via stage design rather than world-state variation.
